data.title: Secunet Security Networks (XTRA:YSN) Margin Stability Supports Bullish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
secunet Security Networks (XTRA:YSN) has opened FY 2025 with first half revenue of €171.7 million and basic EPS of €0.77, against a backdrop of trailing twelve month revenue of €433.8 million and EPS of €4.94 that reflects earnings growth of 15.6% over the past year. The company has seen revenue move from €144.3 million in the first half of 2024 to €262.1 million in the second half and then to €171.7 million in the first half of 2025, while EPS shifted from €0.16 to €4.16 and then €0.77. This progression sets the scene for investors to focus closely on how current margins support those earnings trends.

See our full analysis for secunet Security Networks.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the dominant narratives around growth, quality and risk that investors have been following.

See what the community is saying about secunet Security Networks

Advertisement

Margins Hold Steady Around 7.4%

  • Trailing twelve month net profit margin sits at 7.4%, only slightly above the prior 7.2%, on €433.8 million of revenue and €31.9 million of net income, so profits are broadly tracking revenue rather than breaking away.
  • What stands out for the bullish camp is that this steady margin profile is paired with 15.6% earnings growth over the last year and forecasts for roughly 13.4% annual earnings growth and 9.4% annual revenue growth, which they argue points to operating leverage over time.
    • Bulls point to high quality earnings and a small step up in margin as evidence that growth is not coming from aggressive accounting or one offs, but the margin gap between 7.4% today and higher future targets still needs to be bridged.
    • They also highlight that expected revenue growth is above the cited 6% for the German market, so if margins even edge up from 7.4% the absolute profit pool could increase meaningfully, which is key to justifying a premium valuation.

Bulls argue that steady margins on growing revenue are the foundation for the more optimistic earnings narrative, and that is exactly what the latest 7.4% net margin on €433.8 million of trailing revenue suggests. However, the step up to higher future margins is not in the bag yet, so it is worth checking how that bullish case is built out in detail 🐂 secunet Security Networks Bull Case

Premium P/E Versus DCF Fair Value

  • The shares trade at a P/E of 35.1x compared with a peer average of 18.3x and German IT industry average of 26.7x, while the current price of €173.20 sits above the DCF fair value of €136.61, so the market is paying a clear premium.
  • Skeptics focus on this gap, arguing in the bearish narrative that paying such a premium when the DCF fair value is lower leaves little room for disappointment if revenue grows at closer to 7.6% a year and margins rise only modestly to 7.7%.
    • Bears point out that even with trailing earnings growth of 15.6%, the required future multiples implied by some analyst targets remain high relative to the 35.1x P/E today and the lower peer and industry averages.
    • They also stress that if growth settles nearer the lower end of forecasts, the combination of a P/E premium and price above DCF fair value could weigh on the share price more than on companies priced closer to their intrinsic estimates.

Bears warn that paying 35.1x earnings when DCF fair value is €136.61 and peers sit nearer 18.3x makes the cautious scenario worth understanding in full, especially if growth slows toward the lower end of forecasts 🐻 secunet Security Networks Bear Case

Earnings Trend Versus Five Year History

  • Trailing twelve month earnings have grown 15.6% after an average 9.2% yearly decline over the past five years, which means the latest upswing is sharp compared with the longer term pattern.
  • Analysts' consensus view treats this rebound as part of a broader reset, with revenue expected to rise about 8.9% a year and earnings to reach €48.3 million over the coming years, but the split between more bullish and more cautious forecasts shows that not everyone is convinced this recent growth fully overturns the five year decline.
    • Consensus points to higher expected margins versus the current 7.4%, yet the small gap from last year’s 7.2% means investors still have limited hard evidence that margin expansion is firmly in place.
    • At the same time, the analyst target price of €244.25 is well above the current €173.20 share price, so the market will be watching whether earnings keep tracking closer to the high growth assumptions or slide back toward the longer term average.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Tenable Holdings (TENB) After A 51% One Year Share Price Fall
data.description:
To figure out whether Tenable Holdings might still deserve a spot on your watchlist, it helps to separate emotion from price and look closely at what the current share level really implies about value.

That question feels especially relevant now because the stock is trading at US$17.06, with returns of a 15.8% decline over 7 days, an 11.3% decline over 30 days, a 24.9% decline year to date, a 51.2% decline over 1 year, a 62.8% decline over 3 years, and a 54.0% decline over 5 years.

Recent headlines around Tenable have focused on the broader cybersecurity sector and how investors are reassessing risk and growth expectations. This helps explain why sentiment around the stock has shifted. This mix of sector wide discussion and company specific commentary has put more attention on whether the current price already reflects those concerns or leaves room for a different outcome.

Simply Wall St currently gives Tenable a valuation score of 5 out of 6, and the rest of this article will break down what that means across different valuation methods, before closing with a broader way to think about value that goes beyond any single model.

Find out why Tenable Holdings's -51.2% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Tenable Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts them back to today using a required return, aiming to estimate what the business might be worth right now based on those cash flows.

For Tenable Holdings, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about US$253.4 million. Analysts provide explicit free cash flow estimates for several years, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates further, with projected free cash flow of US$304 million in 2030 and a series of annual projections in between. All figures are expressed in US dollars and then discounted back to today’s value.

On this basis, the model suggests an estimated intrinsic value of US$39.29 per share. Compared with the current share price of US$17.06, the implied intrinsic discount is 56.6%, which points to the stock trading well below this DCF estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Tenable Holdings Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings can be uneven, revenue based metrics such as the P/S ratio are often a useful way to think about value, because sales are usually more stable than profits from year to year.

What investors are really weighing with any multiple is how growth potential and risk compare with the price being paid. Higher growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/S multiple, while slower growth or higher uncertainty usually point to a lower, more conservative range.

Tenable Holdings currently trades on a P/S ratio of 1.96x. That sits below both the Software industry average of 3.34x and the peer group average of 4.15x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 2.93x for Tenable, which reflects factors such as its growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with industry or peers, because it adjusts the multiple to what might be reasonable for Tenable’s own characteristics instead of assuming it should trade in line with a broad group.

With the current 1.96x P/S below the 2.93x Fair Ratio, the shares currently appear undervalued on this metric.

Over the last three reported halves, revenue has moved from €104.6 million in 1H 2024 to €113.2 million in 2H 2024 and then to €117.4 million in 1H 2025, while basic EPS shifted from €1.40 to €1.14 and then €0.96. This sets up a results season where the key question for investors is how much pressure on margins is acceptable given the current earnings profile.

See our full analysis for ADLPartner.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to set these results against the prevailing narratives around growth, profitability and risk to see which stories hold up and which need updating.

Margins Squeezed as Net Profit Shrinks

  • Trailing 12 month net income fell from €12.964 million to €8.345 million while net profit margin moved from 6.2% to 3.6%, so more revenue is now translating into a smaller slice of profit.
  • Critics highlight a bearish angle that weaker profitability can weigh on how dependable the business looks, and the numbers here back up part of that concern:
    • Across the last three trailing periods, revenue stepped up from €208.103 million to €230.614 million while net income declined, so margin pressure is clearly visible in the data.
    • An unstable dividend record, combined with margin compression from 6.2% to 3.6%, gives bears concrete income and payout trends to point to rather than just theory.

Five Year EPS Growth Meets Recent Slowdown

  • Five year earnings growth is cited at 8.3% per year, yet trailing 12 month EPS slipped from €3.263877 to €2.096188, showing that the most recent stretch does not match the longer run pace.
  • What is interesting for a more bullish take is how a solid long run earnings record sits next to a softer recent patch:
    • Across the last three half year periods, basic EPS went from €1.40 to €1.135783 to €0.96, which bulls need to square with the 8.3% five year growth figure highlighted in the data.
    • The company is described as having high quality past earnings, so the recent EPS pattern will be a key test of whether that label still fits if this newer trend continues.

Low P/E and DCF Gap Stand Out

  • The shares trade on a P/E of 11.4x versus 14.5x for the European Media industry and 23.1x for peers, and the provided DCF fair value of €118.72 sits far above the current €24 share price.
  • Bulls argue that this valuation setup points to missed potential, and the figures provide both support for that argument and some clear points of tension:
    • The P/E sitting below both the sector and peer averages fits a bullish claim that the stock is priced more cautiously than comparable names, even after a five year earnings growth rate of 8.3% per year.
    • At the same time, the 79.8% gap between €24 and the DCF fair value of €118.72 depends on cash flow assumptions that must be weighed against the recent margin decline from 6.2% to 3.6%.

data.title: Fermi’s Project Matador Buildout Faces Tenant Gap And Revenue Uncertainty
data.description:
• Fermi has yet to secure a cornerstone tenant for its flagship Project Matador campus, despite progressing permits that target 17 GW of power capacity.
  • The company has made substantial capital commitments and lined up equipment financing, but no anchor customer contract has been confirmed.
  • This gap between infrastructure build out and tenant sign on is raising questions about future revenue visibility and the private grid model.

For investors watching NasdaqGS:FRMI, the lack of an anchor tenant at Project Matador now sits alongside a share price of $5.36 and sharp recent share price moves, including a 25.9% decline over the past week and a 46.2% decline over the past month. Year to date, the stock is down 39.2%. This indicates that the market is already reacting to execution risk around Fermi's large scale plans.

The core issue now is whether Fermi can turn its regulatory and financing groundwork into contracted demand at Project Matador. Until a cornerstone tenant is confirmed, questions around utilization, cash generation and the durability of the private grid thesis are likely to remain central for anyone assessing the long term appeal of NasdaqGS:FRMI.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Fermi by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Fermi.

We've flagged 1 risk for Fermi. See which could impact your investment.

Fermi is pressing ahead with permits and equipment financing for Project Matador while still reporting a full year 2025 net loss of US$486.38 million and no signed cornerstone tenant. That combination puts execution squarely in focus. The company now has an approved 6 GW Clean Air Permit, another 5 GW application filed, and a US$165 million term loan for turbines on top of earlier warehouse facilities, all pointing to a capital intensive build before revenue visibility is clear. For readers, the key question is whether the private grid model tailored to hyperscalers will translate into long term contracts on acceptable terms, or whether the cost of carrying assets and high coupon debt will weigh on equity holders if tenant signings stay slow.

Advertisement

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Large net loss of US$486.38 million in 2025, with the business still in a pre revenue phase and dependent on external financing.
  • ⚠️ Significant capital commitments into permits, turbines and private grid infrastructure without a cornerstone tenant increases the risk that assets are underutilised.
  • 🎁 Regulatory progress, including a 6 GW Clean Air Permit and an additional 5 GW application, gives Fermi a clearer path to build out up to 17 GW of capacity.
  • 🎁 Access to US$165 million of equipment financing and other warehouse facilities provides funding flexibility to secure key long lead time assets for Project Matador.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the most important signals will be any announcement of a long term power or data center agreement at Project Matador, especially with large cloud or AI players that currently buy capacity from utilities and colocation providers. Investors may also want to track Fermi's cash runway, debt covenants under the Beal Credit Agreement, and progress on the Nuclear Regulatory Commission review. Together, those factors will influence how much room Fermi has to keep building while it works to convert interest from potential tenants into signed contracts.

data.title: Is It Time To Reconsider Disney (DIS) After Recent Streaming And Parks Updates?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Walt Disney at around US$94.32 is a bargain or a value trap, you are not alone. That is exactly what this article will help you unpack.
  • The stock has seen a 3.7% decline over the last 7 days, an 11.1% decline over 30 days, a 15.7% decline year to date, and a 3.4% decline over the past year. This naturally raises questions about potential opportunity or shifting risk perceptions.
  • Recent headlines have focused on Walt Disney's content pipeline, theme park operations, and ongoing moves in streaming. Investors now have fresh information to weigh against the share price. These updates provide important context for the recent pullbacks and how the market is reacting to the company’s evolving business mix.
  • With a valuation score of 5 out of 6 checks for being undervalued, the company looks interesting on traditional metrics. The next sections will walk through the main valuation approaches, followed by a more complete way to think about what the stock might be worth.

Find out why Walt Disney's -3.4% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Approach 1: Walt Disney Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today’s value using an appropriate rate. It focuses on cash that could theoretically be returned to shareholders over time.

For Walt Disney, the latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $8.6b. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts and extrapolated estimates point to projected Free Cash Flow of about $14.1b in 2030, with a series of annual forecasts between 2026 and 2035 supplied by analysts and Simply Wall St’s own extrapolations.

Pulling these projections together and discounting them back to today gives an estimated intrinsic value of around $98.79 per share. Compared with the current share price of about $94.32, the DCF suggests the stock trades at roughly a 4.5% discount. This indicates a mild gap rather than a huge disconnect.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Walt Disney is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Walt Disney.

Approach 2: Walt Disney Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful yardstick because it links what you pay for each share to the earnings that business is currently generating. It gives you a quick sense of how many dollars of price the market is asking for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E often reflects how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually means a lower one.

Walt Disney currently trades on a P/E of about 13.64x. That sits below the Entertainment industry average of about 35.11x and well below the peer group average of roughly 61.15x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework, which estimates what a more tailored P/E might look like after considering earnings growth, industry, profit margin, market cap and risks, points to a Fair Ratio of about 24.91x.

This Fair Ratio is more specific to Walt Disney than simple peer or industry comparisons because it adjusts for company level characteristics rather than assuming one size fits all. Comparing the current P/E of 13.64x with the Fair Ratio of 24.91x indicates that the shares appear undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED
